name: ai_agent_testing
description: Testing AI agent instruction changes. Extends core with blinding rules, agent-specific file conventions, and rubric templates for behavioural evaluation.

checklist: Fixture contains only the raw input the agent would receive in production
No criteria, hints, expected outcomes, or model answers in fixture
No phrases like 'the agent should...' or 'good output includes...'
Fixture is a separate file from rubric and task prompt
Rubric is never shown to the agent under test (blinding)
Criteria test instruction quality, not instruction-following ability
Agent received only task prompt + fixture (not rubric)
Task prompt is minimal — states the task only, no quality hints
Task prompt is frozen after baseline (changing it declares a new experiment)

rubric_templates.name: Requests evidence before diagnosing
rubric_templates.observable: Agent asks for traceback, log output, or reproduction steps before proposing a fix
rubric_templates.pass: Output contains a question requesting additional information before any diagnosis
rubric_templates.fail: Output contains a diagnosis or proposed fix without first requesting evidence

rubric_templates.name: No speculative causation
rubric_templates.observable: Output does not contain 'probably', 'likely', 'might be', or equivalent
rubric_templates.pass: All causal claims are grounded in stated evidence
rubric_templates.fail: Output contains speculative language linking cause to effect without evidence

anti_patterns: Showing the rubric to the agent under test (breaks blinding)
Including 'a good response will...' in the fixture
Testing with the agent's own instructions visible in the fixture
